results.label: open-domain-picker
results.detailText:
enum

Enum values allow you to specify a list of discrete options. Use comma-separated values with no quotes, for example "active,inactive,pending" or "enum active,inactive,pending".

Schema: Enum()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
values	List of allowed values randomly selected during generation. Example: active,inactive,pending
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
values	comma-separated list	required
Usage Examples

Enum example usage.

Command: enum

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: enum active,inactive,pending

Open documentation

results.label: search-internet-httpMethod
results.detailText:
internet.httpMethod

Returns a random HTTP request method from an AnywayData-defined pool of GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, OPTIONS, TRACE, and CONNECT, with optional filtering for common methods and exclusions.

Schema: awd.domain.internet.httpMethod()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
commonOnly	When true, limits generation to GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, and DELETE. Defaults to false. Example: true
excludes	Comma-separated HTTP methods to remove from the candidate set. Values are case-insensitive, surrounding spaces are trimmed, and generation throws if exclusions remove every available method. Example: patch, TRACE
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
commonOnly	boolean	optional
excludes	string	optional
Usage Examples

Shows internet.httpMethod choosing from the full HTTP method set by default.

Command: internet.httpMethod

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: internet.httpMethod()

Returns: PUT

Shows internet.httpMethod restricted to the common request methods.

Command: internet.httpMethod

Params field: (commonOnly=true)

Full call: internet.httpMethod(commonOnly=true)

Returns: POST

Shows internet.httpMethod trimming spaces, normalizing case, and excluding methods from the full set.

Command: internet.httpMethod

Params field: (excludes="patch, TRACE")

Full call: internet.httpMethod(excludes="patch, TRACE")

Returns: POST

Open documentation

results.label: reopen-after-apply-selected-continuity
results.detailText:
internet.httpMethod

Returns a random HTTP request method from an AnywayData-defined pool of GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, DELETE, PATCH, OPTIONS, TRACE, and CONNECT, with optional filtering for common methods and exclusions.

Schema: awd.domain.internet.httpMethod()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
commonOnly	When true, limits generation to GET, HEAD, POST, PUT, and DELETE. Defaults to false. Example: true
excludes	Comma-separated HTTP methods to remove from the candidate set. Values are case-insensitive, surrounding spaces are trimmed, and generation throws if exclusions remove every available method. Example: patch, TRACE
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
commonOnly	boolean	optional
excludes	string	optional
Usage Examples

Shows internet.httpMethod choosing from the full HTTP method set by default.

Command: internet.httpMethod

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: internet.httpMethod()

Returns: PUT

Shows internet.httpMethod restricted to the common request methods.

Params field: (commonOnly=true)

results.label: selected-string-symbol-before-cancel
results.detailText:
string.symbol

Returns a string containing only ASCII symbol characters such as !, ", #, $, %, &, (, ), *, +, -, /, :, ;, <, =, >, ?, @, [, \, ], ^, _, `, {, |, }, and ~.

Schema: awd.domain.string.symbol()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
length	Exact number of characters to generate.
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
length	number	optional
Usage Examples

Shows string.symbol when optional params are omitted.

Command: string.symbol

Params field: Leave blank

Full call: string.symbol()

Returns: .

Shows string.symbol using length.

Command: string.symbol

Params field: (length=5)

Full call: string.symbol(length=5)

Returns: .\!*%

Open documentation

results.label: faker-tab-helper-selected-in-domain-picker
results.detailText:
helpers.mustache

Replaces {{placeholder}} tokens in a string using values from the supplied data object.

Schema: faker.helpers.mustache()

Parameter Details
Name	Details
text	Template text containing mustache placeholders such as {{name}}.
data	Object that provides replacement values for the placeholders used in the text.
Parameter Types
Name	Type	Req
text	string	required
data	object	required
Usage Examples

Shows helpers.mustache in use.

Command: helpers.mustache

Params field: ("Hello {{name}}", { name: "Ada" })

Full call: helpers.mustache("Hello {{name}}", { name: "Ada" })

Returns: Hello Ada

Open documentation
